description:
" GST Hold Report format 
Ravi Shankar Dubey 

# GST Hold Development - Additional Requirements

1. Overview

The GST Hold functionality is now live and working as expected. The user has started using it and requires additional enhancements, mainly related to reporting and usability improvements. Below are the key requirements:

2. Reports Requirement

The user requires specific reports related to GST Hold.These reports should be available for download from the Odoo portal.

3. GST Payment Reflection in Reports

Once a GST payment is made (either partially or fully), it should be reflected in the reports.When the user uploads the reports to the Odoo portal, the payment status should update accordingly.

4. Purpose of Reason & Comments Column

If an entry is under GST Hold, the reason for the hold should be captured.The reason should be selected from a predefined dropdown list.The dropdown list should contain the reasons specified in the attached Excel file.

This reason should also be included in the generated reports.

5. Commenting Without Moving to Draft Mode

Users should be able to add a comment to a GST Hold entry without changing its status to draft.

The comment field should allow users to enter additional details regarding the GST Hold.

These comments should also appear in the reports.

6. Removing GST Hold Comments

If a vendor resolves the GST issue, the user should be able to remove the comment without moving the entry to draft mode.

This will allow for a smooth transition without disrupting existing entries.

7. Expected Outcome

The reports should correctly reflect GST payments.The reason for GST Hold should be selectable from a dropdown and should be visible in reports.Users should be able to add and remove comments without affecting the entry status."

Format Below -

title: GST Hold Reporting and Usability Enhancements

description:
Delivery note  Customer invoice


Currently, the "Delivery Note" in customer invoices is a free-text field, which makes report extraction difficult. To improve usability and reduce manual intervention, we propose the following changes:

Invoice Line-Level Delivery Notes: Instead of a single text field, allow users to add delivery notes at the invoice line level.
Optional Hide for Existing Field: Keep the existing text field as optional (hidden by default) so users can enable it if necessary.
Improved Report Generation: With delivery notes structured at the line level, generating reports will be more efficient.
title: Line-Level Delivery Notes for Customer Invoices

description:
Automating Interbranch Transactions for Employee Expenses

Need to enable automated interbranch transaction posting for employee expenses. Here's the scenario:

1.In Branch A (Karnataka), a cash payment is made to an employee for expenses that will be incurred in Branch B (Andhra Pradesh).
2.Currently, this results in Branch A showing a receivable balance and Branch B showing a payable balance for the same employee.
3.To simplify account reconciliation, we require an automated adjustment entry to close the employee’s account seamlessly.
Proposed Solution:
1.Implement a window-type mechanism (as discussed with Lepeesh) where users can enter reimbursements for multiple projects and states.
2.The system should allow balancing all reimbursements with a single payment (e.g., from Karnataka).
3.This should trigger automated interbranch journal entries to clear receivables/payables across branches.
title: Automated Interbranch Expense Posting and Reconciliation

description:
" 
Payment Request Data Duplication Logic:

    Selectively copy predefined, relevant data fields (e.g., vendor details, line items, payment terms, amounts, as applicable to the payment request type).

    The specific list of fields to be copied should be clearly defined and, ideally, configurable per payment request type to prevent erroneous data carryover.

New Payment Request Attributes:

    Creator: Set to the current user performing the duplication.
    Creation Timestamp: Set to the current system date and time.
    Identifier: Assign a new, unique document ID.
    Workflow: Initiate a new, independent approval workflow from the beginning.

Scope of Application:

    The feature must support all defined payment request types, including (but not limited to):
        Payment requests against Purchase Orders (POs).
        Non-PO payment requests (e.g., advances, direct invoices, retentions, GST on hold payments)."""

description:
Excel report  visibility
When we download Excel reports (like General Ledger, Partner Ledger, Journal Entries):
The data should come in a true tabular / columnar format
Meaning: each data point should be in its own column, and rows should repeat properly
Not as “one row with multiple headings” or complex merged cells

They want:

A tabular download — so that data can be filtered, pivoted, or analysed easily
The downloaded data should look similar to what they see on the screen in Odoo
Not just the fixed set of standard columns decided at the backend
This approach should apply to:
1.General Ledger
2.Partner Ledger
3.General Items
4.Journal Entries

All possible columns should be available in the view - Users can choose which ones to show/hide and then download

Example Attached:

They shared two sheets:
First sheet → how it currently downloads (flat, non-tabular, less usable)
Second sheet → how they want it to download (clean columns, easy to filter)

In short - Make Excel downloads truly columnar & tabular, matching what the user sees on screen, and allow users to pick which fields they want

This is to help them do analysis, pivots, and easier reporting

Reference or example - https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1W6QhedVDqSF4Yi3gSqcky6ifJa9YDR-f/edit?gid=843401382#gid=843401382
title: Standardized Tabular Excel Exports for Accounting Reports

description:
Concern on LDC Development
Currently, the setup in Vendor Master is designed as one partner = one LDC.
However, this is not correct because in practice, one PAN can have multiple partners (i.e., different GST registrations under the same PAN).

So, the LDC structure should allow:

One PAN - Multiple Partners

Each partner can then be linked with its respective GST, branch, or location.


Tilak input required

Trigger validation on PO line qty update (write).
Check billed qty using purchase.order.line.qty_invoiced (or equivalent).
If qty_invoiced >= product_qty and user tries to increase product_qty → block.

Product allowed variance in reciept is the ideal solution.
Since variance is not implemented the control can not be placed strictly 


(This will be adressed, if we implement 3way match)

title: PO Billing Control and GRN Consolidation

description:
⟨⟨⟨GRN BASED BILLING⟩⟩⟩

1. Restrict Bill Creation from PO
If a Purchase Order does not contain any line with product type = Service, users should not be allowed to create a Vendor Bill directly from the Purchase Order (from form view, tree view, or any other action).
In such cases, show warning:
“Please create the bill from GRN/SRN.”
Billing should be allowed only through GRN/SRN for POs containing only storable/consumable products.
 2. Linkage Between GRN and Invoice Lines
Ensure proper linkage between stock.move and account.move.line (invoice line).
Use existing linkage fields if available; otherwise add:
 • Hidden Many2one to stock.move in invoice line
 • Many2one to invoice line in stock.move

 3. Invoiced Quantity Tracking in GRN (stock.move)
Add a float field “Invoiced Qty” (UoM precision) in stock.move (optional/hidden in view).
This field should update automatically with total quantity from draft or posted vendor bills created from that stock move.
 4. Create Bill from GRN Logic
While creating bills from GRN:
 • Billable Qty = Move Qty – Invoiced Qty
 • Only remaining quantity should be available for billing

 5. Validation – Fully Invoiced GRNs
If all stock move lines are fully invoiced, billing should be blocked with warning:
“No lines to invoice.”
 6. Multiple GRN Selection Behaviour
If multiple GRNs are selected for billing:
 • Do not merge stock moves, even if products are the same
 • Each stock move should create a separate invoice line

 7. GRN Reference in Invoice Lines
In Vendor Bills and Vendor Credit Notes (in_invoice / in_refund), add a Many2one field in invoice lines:
 • Field Name: GRN Ref
 • Model: stock.picking
 • Optional (hide)
 • Should store the GRN from which that invoice line was created.
title: GRN Based Billing Implementation and Validation Logic

description:
HDFC schduler failed  to update the payment status 

RCA: High-volume concurrent payment updates cause database deadlocks during sequence generation, resulting in stalled scheduler tasks.When multiple payment threads attempted to update status codes simultaneously, the database sequence generator (used for audit logs/ID generation) applied a global table lock. This forced subsequent threads into an indefinite "Awaiting" state, creating a processing bottleneck where no new records could be updated until the initial lock was manually or system-cleared.


fix: The HDFC scheduler must process payment status updates independently, ensuring a single transaction failure or lock does not halt the entire batch.
title: HDFC Scheduler Database Deadlock Resolution

description:
we will need to introduce Validity (Start Date and End Date) on the Tax. 
Additionally, a validation should be implemented in action_post of account.move to check the validity of the taxes applied on its lines.

Validation Logic:
If an End Date is defined on the Tax, and the Accounting Date of the journal entry is later than the Tax’s End Date:
Warning:
“The tax ‘{tax_name}’ is no longer valid on the selected date.”

If a Start Date is defined on the Tax, and the Accounting Date of the journal entry is earlier than the Tax’s Start Date:
Warning:
“The tax ‘{tax_name}’ is not yet valid on the selected date.”
title: Tax Validity Period and Date Verification Warnings
